purpose: Governed, typed-slot templates for a small allow-listed set of built-in AutoCAD commands that have no safe path today because the only way to invoke a built-in command is through the SAFETY_FORBIDDEN raw command-dispatch surface (see docs/GOVERNED_COMMAND_TEMPLATES.md). Never write_original. Every slot value is validated before it ever reaches a .scr file; no free-text slot ever reaches the command line.

templates.summary: Run the built-in AUDIT command against the staged drawing; optionally fix detected errors.

write_mode.note: The engine ALWAYS issues _QSAVE against the STAGED (disposable) copy before QUIT, regardless of write_mode -- a root-caused fix for an accoreconsole exit hang (see the fix_answer slot note + design doc section 5). This does not change the write_mode CONTRACT: 'read' still means the ORIGINAL is never touched and no persistence is reported/guaranteed to the caller; only the throwaway staged copy gets flushed so accoreconsole's exit path has nothing pending to hang on.

fix_answer.description: Answer to AUDIT's 'Fix any errors detected? [Yes/No]' prompt. Both values live-verified (Lane W5-TMPL). 'N' was ORIGINALLY found to hang accoreconsole on process exit (4/4 trials) even though the AUDIT command itself and the postcondition entity-count probe both completed correctly before the hang -- root-caused: accoreconsole hangs on QUIT whenever the in-memory database has ANY unsaved modification relative to its last save point (reproduced independently with a bare LINE command and with ARRAYRECT, both fixed the same way). Fix: the engine now unconditionally QSAVEs the staged copy before QUIT (see write_mode.note above); 'N' now exits cleanly. See docs/GOVERNED_COMMAND_TEMPLATES.md section 5 for the full root-cause narrative.

postconditions.description: AUDIT's own summary line. Live-measured on this machine's Korean-locale AutoCAD 2027 (accoreconsole stdout is Korean UI text, not English) -- pattern is locale-specific; an English-locale AutoCAD install would need the English form 'Total (\d+) errors? found[.,]? (\d+) (?:were |was )?fixed' added as an alternative.
Modelspace entity count must be unchanged by AUDIT (audit repairs symbol-table/dictionary issues; it does not add/remove entities on a clean fixture).

templates.notes: Handle-stability caveat (per the 2026-07-06 SDK census re-audit, D:\dev\.build\cados_plan\sdk_census_reaudit_20260706.md): AUDIT is one of a small class of operations (alongside RECOVER and BIND) flagged as a silent handle-churn trap -- AutoCAD does not guarantee that entity handles are preserved across an AUDIT pass, even a fix=Y pass that reports 0 errors fixed. This template's postcondition checks entity COUNT stability only, not per-handle identity; a caller that depends on a specific entity's handle surviving an AUDIT call should verify that separately via the identity-contract lane (W5-ID) rather than assume it here.

templates.summary: Run the built-in -PURGE command (all unused named objects, no per-item confirmation) against the staged drawing.

postconditions.description: First purged-named-object deletion line, if any (purged-item report). Korean-locale text, live-measured; not required (a clean drawing with nothing to purge legitimately produces zero matches).
PURGE removes only unused named objects (layers/linetypes/styles/blocks not referenced by any entity); modelspace entity count must be unchanged.

templates.notes: verify_each is hardcoded literal 'N', never an agent-controllable slot: answering 'Y' opens an unbounded per-item confirmation loop with no headless-safe way to answer an unknown number of further prompts -- exactly the kind of hazard this registry exists to close off, not merely make agent-configurable.

templates.summary: Create an associative rectangular array (ARRAYRECT) from the drawing's last-created entity, via the explicit Count/Spacing/eXit command sub-options (never the dynamic corner-point default, which is ambiguous headless).

row_spacing.description: Distance between rows (drawing units; ARRAYRECT's Spacing sub-option).

col_spacing.description: Distance between columns (drawing units; ARRAYRECT's Spacing sub-option).

postconditions.description: Live-measured (Lane W5-TMPL): an associative array wraps its source entity as a SINGLE selectable object (visually rows*cols copies, but ssget/sslength counts it as 1 -- the array does not net-add entities to the database). Entity count must be unchanged.

templates.notes: v1 scope limitation (documented, not hidden): selects via AutoCAD's 'L' (Last) option -- the drawing's most-recently-created entity in database order, NOT an agent-addressable handle. A handle-based selection slot (LISP (handent "...") + (sssetfirst ...) pre-selection) is a natural follow-up, not built by this lane. Mapped from the census's DCM/associativity command-coverage estimate (D:\dev\.build\cados_plan\sdk_census_reaudit_20260706.md section 5): AcDbAssocArrayActionBody::createInstance's real command-line equivalent. The census scoped its 16-17/23 estimate to 'attended full_autocad'; this lane found the ACTUAL blocker was a general accoreconsole exit-hang (see maintenance.drawing.audit's fix_answer note + design doc section 5), root-caused and fixed at the engine level (unconditional _QSAVE before QUIT) -- ARRAYRECT itself runs headless cleanly once that fix is applied, contradicting the implicit 'needs attended' assumption for at least this one op.

templates.summary: Release associativity on the drawing's last associative array (EXPLODE), converting it back into independent entities.

postconditions.description: Live-measured (Lane W5-TMPL): exploding an associative array converts its single wrapping entity into rows*cols independent entities -- entity count must increase (measured exact case: a 3x2 array, 21747 -> 21752, +5 = rows*cols-1). The exact delta is array-size-dependent and not asserted here; only the direction (increase) is a safe, general invariant.

templates.notes: v1 scope limitation (documented, not hidden): 'L' (Last) selection only, same as define.assocarray.rectangular -- see that template's notes. EXPLODE's own 'Select objects:' sub-loop auto-terminates on a single 'L' answer (measured: does NOT need a trailing blank line the way ARRAYRECT/COPY's selection loops do; an extra blank line here fires EXPLODE a second time on garbage input -- see docs/GOVERNED_COMMAND_TEMPLATES.md section 5 for the exact failure trace this avoids).
